name: Minoa
startIntroPart1: The Lilies and the bliss wash over you oh Priestesses, your people have some offerings, your Goddess said, the offering is a proud kingdom of traders and artisans centred on the island of Crete, away from the petty and bloody squabbles of the main Lander around you. In this perpetual Mediterranean bliss and Peace your people blossomed, becoming the most culturally and technologically advanced empire of the age. The tales of your prosperity spread across the isles of the Aegean through your many trade routes and yet somehow you Priestesses remain forgotten in the Palaces influencing Crete from the shadows. But, the Sun and The Serpent watch over you, oh, Priestesses, mythical and Enigmatic Priestesses of Knossos, Matriarchs of Crete, Mothers of Greece. Contemporaries of the Ancient Egyptians, your people were the foremost naval power in the Levant for hundreds of years. The tales of your exploits on Earth fell into mystery, and only clues remain as to the extent of your power. Did you Priestesses truly demand human sacrifice of Athens? Were the caverns under your palace the twisted labryinth of legend? We will carry your secrets to the underworld, oh, mighty and blessed Priestesses.
startIntroPart2: Oh, daughters of the Mother Goddess, you come to this new world as its Queen-Priestess! The power of the Earthshaker is broken, and we live no longer in the shadow of his fiery wrath! Will you rebuild the glorious metropolis of Knossos? Will your navy bring the sophistication of Crete to every corner of this uncultured world? Oh Priestesses, The Minoan empire has long since fallen and King Minos is dead, your Goddesses is gone, and yet men continue to sing tales of glory to which your kingdom and empire was the setting without singing also your blessed names. It is time to raise that kingdom anew and reclaim your names! Will you spur your merchants and civilians to rebuild their ships and palaces, reclaiming Crete? Will your palaces and pottery once again be the envy of all the nations around you? Will you build a civilization that will stand the test of time?
declaringWar: The Bulls, Mother Goddess, Snake Goddess, almost everyone has spoken, you digs your claws too deep into our sophisticated Palaces. Thus, it is time for retribution.
attacked: War? In this hour? At this time? Do you really wish to inflict much pain to your people and future descendants? If so then I guess, that'll do.
defeated: No, this can't be... WHAT ARE YOU LOOKING AT!? LEAVE!! Enjoy your Victory... But hear this, the Snake Goddess will always find a way to eat your little, despicable, soul.
introduction: It has been Prophesied today that a new visitor may come. From the looks of it. It was you, and As Chief Priestess I welcome you, to the Palace of Knossos... Dismissed.
neutralLetsHearIt: Can we perhaps assist with something?
neutralNo: Perhaps, but, not exactly what we're looking for.
neutralYes: May Mother Goddess bless your land with bountiful harvest.
The Snake Goddess is very pleased with your offerings.
hateHello: Greetings, the Chief Priestess is very busy. In her place, I shall entertain you instead.
hateNo: Do you so wish to anger the Snake Goddess?
I don't think you're taking this meeting as seriously as I did.
Are you thinking clearly, just now?
afterPeace: Very well. My dear, it's just such shame, that the Snake Goddess doesn't have the chance to taste your skull and soul, You know.
tradeRequest: The traders has just came back from their missions abroad, and when they return to Knossos, they were offering some unheard things to the Goddesses. Would you enlighten us in such matters of object?
denounced: The Priestesses remembers and was not caring about matters that was spewed from an insignificant barbarians. Just so you know.
declaringfriendship: The Bulls, the Goddesses, my people is very pleased with you and your people. Please, if we may, let's just let both of our people to live as companions.
uniqueText: The Minoans Starts the game with [Sailing]. The Minoans got +50% more yield from every [Fishing Boats] in [Coastal] cities, The Minoans also got +70% more yield from [Farm] built in [Coastal] tiles while [Plantation] in [Coastal] tiles got +60% more yield. Every [Pasture] owned by Minoa produce +1 [Gold] While [Cattle] produce +1 [Culture] and [Faith], all those bonuses is also tripled when those improvement is built in [Coastal] tiles. 38% Food will be carried over after population increases in all cities connected to capital. If [Harbor] is constructed in all [Coastal] cities, each Trade Route will begun to produce [+3 Culture], +3 [Food], +3 [Gold], and +2 [Science].
uniques: [38]% Food is carried over after population increases [in all cities connected to capital]
[+3 Culture, +3 Food, +3 Gold, +2 Science] from each Trade Route <if [Harbor] is constructed in all [Coastal] cities>
